content.full_text:
Z. Harry Astor
Astor & Phillips
Suite 1000, Penthouse
505 City Parkway West
Orange, CA 92668-2983

July 16, 1992

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-355

Dear Mr. Astor:

You have requested advice on behalf of your lobbying firm and
your client, CR&R Incorporated (CR&R) and its wholly owned
subsidiary Mobile Recycling Corporation (Mobile), concerning the
lobbying disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTION

You have asked what additional information or documents are
needed so your firm's registration properly reflects the clients
on whose behalf it lobbies.

CONCLUSION

The Secretary of State's office requires clarification of the
status of CR&R and Mobile Recycling Corp. If CR&R and Mobile are
parent and subsidiary, they must amend the joint authorization
filed in January 1992 to clarify that they are filing as parent
and subsidiary and to provide the effective date of that change.
Mobile also must amend the individual authorization it has filed
for another lobbying firm to include CR&R as its parent entity or,
if the other firm no longer lobbies on its behalf, Mobile must
take whatever steps are necessary to be removed from that firm's
registration.

_________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 80 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Astor, Z. Harry
No. A-92-355
Page 2


FACTS

A review of the statements filed with the Secretary of State
indicates that your firm's initial registration (Form 601) for the
91-92 legislative session showed that you were lobbying on behalf
of CR&R as of January 1, 1991. Mobile authorized another lobbying
firm to lobby on its behalf as of June 1, 1991. On January 30,
1992, a lobbying firm authorization (Form 602) was filed with the
Secretary of State authorizing Astor & Phillips to lobby on behalf
of CR&R Inc. and Mobile Recycling Corporation. On April 27, 1992,
your firm amended its registration indicating the addition of CR&R -
and its Wholly Owned Subsidiary, Mobile Recycling Corp. as a
client.

The Office of the Secretary of State indicates that they have
been informed orally that Mobile was acquired by or merged into
CR&R. However, this relationship has not been confirmed in
writing, and Mobile is apparently still registered as an
individual client for another firm.

ANALYSIS

The Secretary of State must receive an authorization (Form
602) which describes the relationship between CR&R and Mobile, and
provides the effective date when Mobile became a subsidiary of CR&R.
The status of Mobile's relationship with the other lobbying firm
must also be clarified. Once these matters are resolved, Mobile's
lobbying activities may be included in the reports filed by CR&R.

The Secretary of State has also informed us that CR&R has not
filed the lobbyist employer reports (Form 635) required under
sections 86115 and 86116 since the first quarter of 1991, and
Mobile has not filed since the second quarter of 1991. The
Secretary of State's records also show that notices were sent to
CR&R and Mobile indicating that those quarterly statements have
not been filed.

Section 86115 provides that:

Subject to the exceptions in Section 86300, the following
persons shall file the statements required by Section 86116:
(a) Any lobbyist employer; and
(b) Any person who directly or indirectly makes
payments to influence legislative or administrative action of
five thousand dollars ($5,000) or more in value in any
calendar quarter, unless all of the payments are of the type
described in subdivision (c) of Section 82045.

Section 86117 provides that:

(a) Reports required by Sections 86114 and 86116 shall
be filed during the month following each calendar quarter.
The period covered shall be from the beginning of the

Astor, Z. Harry
No. A-92-355
Page 3


calendar year through the last day of the quarter prior to
the month during which the report is filed, except as
specified in subdivision (b), and except that the period
covered shall not include any information reported in
previous reports filed by the same person....

     (b)  The period covered by the first report a person is
required to file pursuant to Sections 86114 and Section 86116
shall begin with the first day of the calendar quarter in
which the filer first registered or qualified.  On the first
report a person is required to file, the total amount shall
be stated for the entire calendar quarter covered by the
first report.

     All late statements are subject to fines of $10 per day for
each day the statements are late.  Liability need not be enforced
if on an impartial basis the filing officer determines that the
late filing was not willful and that enforcement of the fine will
not further the purposes of the Act.  However, if a statement is
not filed within 10 days after the filing officer has sent
specific written notice of the filing requirement, the filing
officer has no authority to grant a waiver of the liability.
(Section 91013.)

     A copy of this letter is being forwarded to the Secretary of
State.  If your clients do not file their late statements with the
Secretary of State within 10 days, this matter will be referred to
the Commission's Enforcement Division.  You may also want to
remind your clients that lobbyist employer reports for the second
quarter of 1992 are due by July 31, 1992.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: Wayne P. Imberi
                                            Political Reform Consultant

cc:  Secretary of State
     Attn:  Political Reform Division
